weight_transform: If torch weight is a Python scalar, pass directly as second arg. If torch nn.PReLU module, extract module.weight (shape [C] or [1]) and pass as 1-D weight; convert via ttnn.from_torch(weight, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device) when passing as tensor. No transposition or repacking required.
applicability_notes: torch_pattern_notes: Equivalent decomposition replaced by single op: torch.where(x >= 0, x, weight * x). Weight may be a scalar, a 1-D per-channel tensor, or broadcastable tensor; golden coerces non-tensor weight via torch.tensor(weight, dtype=input.dtype).; ttnn_replacement: ttnn.prelu(input_tensor_a, input_tensor_b); constraints: {'dtype_tested': ['bfloat16'], 'value_range_tested': '[-100, 100]', 'pcc_threshold': 0.999, 'weight_forms': ['tensor', 'scalar', '1D per-channel list']}

placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.from_torch(in_data1, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device) with default (DRAM interleaved) memory config

placement_observations.size_descriptor: same shape as input_a, or scalar, or 1-D [C]
placement_observations.source: unit test: ttnn.from_torch(in_data2, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device) with default (DRAM interleaved) memory config; scalar/list variants passed host-side
